rule_schema.standard_reference: Exact section or rule reference in standards/testing.md.
rule_schema.section: Human section name from the standard.
rule_schema.title: Short operational title.
rule_schema.requirement: Normative requirement extracted from the standard.
rule_schema.applies_to: Repo surfaces where the rule applies.
rule_schema.trigger_kinds: Change categories that should pull this rule into an applicable checklist.
rule_schema.phases: Workflow phases where this rule is relevant.
rule_schema.enforcement_mode: validator-required, checklist-required, checklist-and-validator, advisory, or backlog.
rule_schema.evidence_required: What an implementation or verification receipt should record when applicable.
rule_schema.automation: Current or intended mechanical enforcement owner.

rules.title: Use the app's existing package manager and scripts
rules.requirement: Use the package manager and scripts already present in each app.
evidence_required: Verification used the package manager already present in the app.
Verification used existing package scripts rather than invented commands.

rules.title: Run the standard app check sequence when available
rules.requirement: Run typecheck, lint, test, build, and e2e when those scripts are present in the app.
evidence_required: Verification records results for typecheck, lint, test, build, and e2e where present.
The handoff distinguishes passed checks from checks that were not available.

rules.title: Report missing scripts explicitly
rules.requirement: If a script is missing, report it. Do not claim it passed.
evidence_required: Missing package scripts are listed as missing or skipped with the exact script name.
No final summary claims a missing script passed.

rules.title: Verify rendered UI for UI work
rules.requirement: For UI work, builds are not enough. Verify the rendered app.
evidence_required: UI work records rendered verification evidence beyond a successful build.
Verification identifies the rendered surface or flow that was checked.

rules.title: Check app load, overlays, console, and interaction
rules.requirement: Rendered UI verification checks that the app loads to a meaningful screen, no framework error overlay appears, the console has no relevant errors, and a core user interaction changes UI state.
evidence_required: Rendered verification confirms a meaningful loaded screen.
Verification records absence of framework overlays and relevant console errors.
At least one core interaction is exercised when UI behavior changed.

rules.title: Verify desktop and mobile layout quality
rules.requirement: Rendered UI verification checks desktop and mobile viewport layouts and confirms text and controls do not overlap or clip.
evidence_required: Rendered verification covers at least one desktop viewport and one mobile viewport.
Verification records that text and controls do not overlap or clip.

rules.title: Prefer Browser plugin and use Playwright as fallback
rules.requirement: Use the Browser plugin when available. Use Playwright as fallback.
evidence_required: Rendered verification identifies whether Browser plugin or Playwright was used.
If neither rendered path could run, the blocker is recorded.

rules.title: Verify web build before Capacitor wrapper checks
rules.requirement: For Capacitor projects, verify web build first, then native wrapper configuration.
evidence_required: Capacitor verification records a successful web build before native wrapper checks.
Native wrapper configuration checks are reported separately from web build checks.

rules.title: Scope native emulator or device checks to native behavior
rules.requirement: Native emulator or device checks should be scoped to the feature requiring native behavior.
evidence_required: Native emulator or device validation is tied to the native behavior changed or required.
If native behavior was not touched, the handoff states why native emulator or device checks were not run.
